info.title: Altur API
info.description: An API aimed to allow third-party developers to interact with the Altur platform through a RESTful interface.

get.description: Returns all Campaigns for your organization

parameters.name: agentId
parameters.description: Filters the Campaigns by Agent ID of the agent assigned to the Campaign

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filters the Campaigns by the Status of the Campaign

parameters.name: integration
parameters.description: Type of integration for the Campaign

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Bottom date of the range to filter the Campaigns in ISO 8601 format

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Top date of the range to filter the Campaigns in ISO 8601 format

parameters.name: archived
parameters.description: If true, archived Campaigns will be included in the results.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of items per page to return.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Cursor to the next page to return.

post.description: Creates a new Campaign for your organization. Use `integration_type` to select between phone-call and WhatsApp; channel-only fields are rejected when they don't match the chosen type.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional client-generated key (UUID recommended) to make the request idempotent. Replays within the dedup window return the original response.

400.description: Invalid input or unsupported integration type

get.description: Returns the detail for a single Campaign, including its nested agent, integration, cycles config and (when available) analytics snapshot.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the Campaign

patch.description: Partial update of a Campaign. Immutable fields (`agent_id`, `integration_id`, `integration_type`, `cycles.enabled`, `cycles.mode`) cannot be modified after creation and return `IMMUTABLE_FIELD` if included. Finished campaigns are read-only.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the Campaign

400.description: Invalid input, immutable field, cycles not enabled, or scheduled-mode-only field misuse

post.description: Apply a lifecycle action to a Campaign. Valid actions are `activate`, `pause`, `archive` and `resume`. The action is idempotent — applying an action when the campaign is already in the target state is a no-op success.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the Campaign

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Lifecycle action to apply

409.description: Invalid status transition for the requested action

get.description: Returns the Campaign Contacts corresponding to the given identifier

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the Campaign to return the Contacts

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filters the Campaign Contacts by the Status of the Campaign Contact

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of items per page to return.

parameters.name: pageIndex
parameters.description: Page number to the next page to return.

post.description: Batch-creates Campaign Contacts. Up to 1000 contacts per request. Each item is processed independently — a single bad row does not fail the batch — and an aggregate `summary` plus per-item `results` is returned. Use `if_duplicate` to control behavior when a contact already exists in the campaign (`skip` keeps the original, `update` overwrites mutable fields).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the Campaign

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Optional client-generated key (UUID recommended) to make the request idempotent.

get.description: Returns the detail of a single contact within a Campaign.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the Campaign

parameters.name: contact_id
parameters.description: The identifier of the contact within the Campaign

delete.description: Deletes a contact from a Campaign. Only contacts in `queue` or `failed` status can be deleted — contacts further along the lifecycle return `CONTACT_NOT_DELETABLE` (409).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the Campaign

parameters.name: contact_id
parameters.description: The identifier of the contact within the Campaign

409.description: Contact is not in a deletable status

get.description: Returns a paginated list of calls for a specific campaign

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the Campaign to return the Calls

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filters the Campaign Calls by the status of the Call

parameters.name: answeredBy
parameters.description: Filters the Campaign Calls by who answered the call

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Filter calls created after this date in ISO 8601 format

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Filter calls created before this date in ISO 8601 format

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of items per page to return.

parameters.name: pageIndex
parameters.description: Page number to return (0-based).

post.description: Create new EndUser for your organization

get.description: Returns the EndUser corresponding to the given identifier

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the EndUser to return

put.description: Updates the details of an EndUser

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the EndUser to update

requestBody.description: EndUser object that needs to be updated

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the EndUser to delete

get.description: Returns the Thread corresponding to the given identifier

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the Thread to return

post.description: Sends Message to the Thread between a Agent and an EndUser, returns the Message created from the request, the Messages generated in response, and returns information about the Thread between the Agent and EndUser

agent_id.description: Unique identifier of the Agent receiving the Message

end_user_id.description: Unique identifier of the EndUser sending the Message

get.description: Get all Messages in a Thread, ordered from oldest to most recently sent

parameters.name: thread_id
parameters.description: The identifier of the Thread to fetch

200.description: Array containing all Messages in the Thread

agent_id.description: Unique identifier of the Agent that will participate in the Call (formerly Chatbot or Assistant)

phone_number_to.description: E.164 formatted phone number of the EndUser to call

end_user_display_name.description: Display name of the EndUser to call

end_user_context.description: Context of the EndUser to call

initial_message.description: Initial message the Agent will say upon the call being answered

get.description: Returns the Call corresponding to the given identifier

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the Call to return

get.description: Access the recording of a Call in form of a file stream

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The identifier of the Call to fetch

200.description: File stream of the Call recording

status.description: The current status of the Campaign

created_at.description: The date and time when the Campaign was created

agent.description: The agent assigned to the Campaign

retries.description: The number of times the campaign will retry to contact a contact if the call or message fails

template.description: WhatsApp Template assigned to the Campaign (WhatsApp campaigns only)

first_message.description: The first message the Agent will say upon the call being answered (Phone call campaigns only)

concurrency_limit.description: The maximum number of concurrent calls allowed (Phone call campaigns only)

cycle_enabled.description: If enabled the campaign will automatically rerun and re-contact contacts (Phone call campaigns only)

cycle_max_iterations.description: The maximum number of times a campaign will retry to contact a contact if the call fails (Phone call campaigns only)

cycle_cooldown_minutes.description: The cooldown time between cycles of the campaign in minutes (Phone call campaigns only)

cycle_filter_statuses.description: The list of contact statuses to exclude from the campaign on cycles (Phone call campaigns only)

cycle_filter_tags.description: The list of contact tags assigned to the contacts to exclude from the campaign on cycles (Phone call campaigns only)

cycle_current_iteration.description: Current iteration of the Campaign (Phone call campaigns only)

cycle_last_iteration_at.description: Last iteration of the Campaign (Phone call campaigns only)

startTime.description: Start time of the Campaign on Monday

stopTime.description: End time of the Campaign on Monday

active.description: Boolean describing if the Campaign is active on Monday

total_count.description: Total number of Campaigns for the given query. NOTE: The result is cache for 3 minutes.
Total number of Campaign Calls for the given query.

answered_by.description: Who or what answered the Call

created_at.description: Datetime when the Call was created (ISO 8601)

started_at.description: Datetime when the Call was started (ISO 8601)

duration.description: Duration of the Call in seconds

billed_duration.description: Billed duration of the Call in seconds

contact.description: The contact associated with this call

extracted_data.description: Key-value pairs extracted from the call conversation

recording_url.description: URL to fetch the call recording

has_next.description: Whether there are more pages available

next_page.description: Index of the next page (null if no more pages)

previous_page.description: Index of the previous page (null if no previous pages)

total_count.description: Total number of Campaign Contacts for the given query. NOTE: Only calculated in the first page.

CampaignDetail.description: Single Campaign detail with nested agent, integration and cycles config. The `analytics` field is included only when a snapshot is available for the campaign.

integration.description: Nested {id, type} of the integration the Campaign was created on

analytics.description: Analytics snapshot for the Campaign. Shape depends on `integration.type` — see `CampaignAnalyticsPhoneCall` and `CampaignAnalyticsWhatsApp`.

CampaignCyclesConfig.description: Cycles configuration block. Phone-call campaigns only.

enabled.description: Whether cycles are enabled. Immutable after creation.

filter_statuses.description: Contact statuses excluded from the next iteration.

filter_tags.description: Thread tag ids whose contacts are excluded from the next iteration. Input-only — not returned on the detail response.

agent_id.description: `public_id` of the agent that will own this campaign.

integration_id.description: `public_id` of the integration to use. Must belong to `agent_id`.

first_message.description: First message the Agent will say upon the call being answered. Phone-call only.

template_alt.description: Alternate WhatsApp template name. WhatsApp only.

CampaignUpdate.description: Partial update. Any field may be omitted. Immutable fields (`agent_id`, `integration_id`, `integration_type`, `cycles.enabled`, `cycles.mode`) cannot be included.

contact.description: Phone number, normalized to E.164 on insert.

f_id.description: Foreign identifier for the contact in your system.

if_duplicate.description: Behavior when the contact's phone number already exists in this campaign.

error.description: Set when status is `failed`. One of `INVALID_PHONE_NUMBER`, `MISSING_CONTACT`, `SERVER_ERROR`.

CampaignApiError.description: Standard error envelope returned by the v1.0 campaigns API.

field.description: Set when `error` is `IMMUTABLE_FIELD`; identifies the offending field path.

info.description: EndUser's information valuable for interactions with Agents or human agents
EndUser's information valuable for interactions with Agents or human agents

color.description: Color used for in-app display of Tag

updated_at.description: Last time the Thread was updated

active.description: Boolean describing if automatic responses by the Agent are active on this Thread

blocked.description: Boolean describing if Thread is blocked

answered_by.description: Who or what answered the Call

created_at.description: Datetime from when the Call was created

started_at.description: Datetime from when the Call was started

ended_at.description: Datetime from when the Call was ended

duration.description: Duration of the Call in seconds

billable_duration.description: Billable duration of the Call in seconds

messages.description: Array of Messages in response to prompted Message

thread_id.description: Unique identifier of the Thread the Message belongs to

media.description: Message's media content, if any (usually in form of URL)

sent_by_user.description: Altur user id (if Message was sent manually by an in-app user)

sent_at.description: Datetime from when the Message was sent

read.description: Boolean describing if the Message is considered as read

DRFBadRequestError.description: Validation error response where each field name that has an error is a property containing an array of error messages

non_field_errors: The date range is not valid.

f_id.description: Unique External identifier for the Campaign Contact

id.description: Unique identifier for the Campaign Contact

retries.description: Voicemail retries of the Campaign Contact

has_follow_up.description: Boolean describing if the Campaign Contact has a follow up scheduled

call_count.description: Number of calls made to the Campaign Contact

billed_duration.description: The total billed duration of the Campaign Contact Calls in seconds

last_call_at.description: The datetime of the last call made to the Campaign Contact

extracted_data.description: Extracted data of the Campaign Contact

tags.description: Tags assigned to the Campaign Contact

CursorPagination.description: Filters are applied to each request independently. Changing filters while paginating may result in gaps or overlaps in the result set. For consistent results, maintain the same filters throughout pagination.

has_next.description: Boolean describing if there are more items to fetch

has_previous.description: Boolean describing if there are previous items to fetch

next_cursor.description: Cursor to the next page to return

previous_cursor.description: Cursor to the previous page to return

PolicyEvaluation.description: Policy compliance evaluation results for a call. Score and count fields will be null when the call has not been evaluated (e.g., not sampled, no active policies, or evaluation still pending). The policies array will be empty in these cases.

score.description: Compliance score from 0 to 10. Formula: (policies_met / applicable_policies) * 10. Null if no applicable policies or evaluation not run.

policies_met_count.description: Number of policies that were met

policies_not_applicable_count.description: Number of policies marked as not applicable

completed.description: Whether the policy evaluation has been completed

OffsetPagination.description: Filters are applied to each request independently. Changing filters while paginating may result in gaps or overlaps in the result set. For consistent results, maintain the same filters throughout pagination.

has_next.description: Boolean describing if there are more items to fetch

has_previous.description: Boolean describing if there are previous items to fetch

next_page.description: Page Index to the next page to return. Can be null if there are no more pages.

previous_page.description: Page Index to the previous page to return. Can be null if there are no previous pages.

apiKeyAuth.name: Authorization
apiKeyAuth.description: Add `api-key YOUR_API_SECRET_KEY` as the value of the `Authorization` header.
